document.title: End-to-End Purchase Coverage Matrix
document.description: Answer four questions without flattening purchase into one linear journey:

readingGuide.startHere: Use purchaseJourney for the overall flow, then filter records by category. Open a purchase trace to follow one complete route step by step.
howToReadARecord: title and summary explain the behavior in plain language
status says how strongly the behavior is currently proven
details contains additional conditions or effects
evidence points to the source that supports the behavior
location shows where the record came from in the Markdown source

purchaseJourney.name: Entry
purchaseJourney.question: Where did the buyer or seller begin or resume?

purchaseJourney.name: Configure
purchaseJourney.question: Which quantity, seat, variant, package, or option applies?

purchaseJourney.name: Identity
purchaseJourney.question: Is the customer a guest, logged in, or interrupted by authentication?

purchaseJourney.name: Basket
purchaseJourney.question: How is inventory selected, held, and validated?

purchaseJourney.name: Checkout
purchaseJourney.question: Which review, delivery, question, and agreement steps appear?

purchaseJourney.name: Payment
purchaseJourney.question: Which tender and payment processor complete the charge?

purchaseJourney.name: Finalize
purchaseJourney.question: How does the purchase command or provider callback create the sale?

purchaseJourney.name: Outcome
purchaseJourney.question: Which invoice, ticket, membership, credit, or other artifact is issued?

purchaseJourney.name: Post-purchase
purchaseJourney.question: What can happen after purchase, such as refund, transfer, resale, or settlement?

statusStates.meaning: Supported by the mapped source or marked current.
Mapped partially or still requires environment-backed proof.
Invalid, unreachable, unsupported, or expected to be rejected.
Deprecated, dormant, historical, or retained for compatibility.
The source row does not provide an explicit status.

content.text: Living, source-backed replacement for the purchase-flow Draw.io diagram.

> [!info] Source map complete; execution gaps remain > Reachable unique behavior branches are mapped from backend rules and frontend reachability. **Source-mapped/Verified** does not mean every combination has run live. Provider, device, worker, race, rendered-artifact, and deployment-only proof remains explicitly listed in Open Gaps.
Answer four questions without flattening purchase into one linear journey:
Where can a buyer or seller start a purchase?
Which item, identity, configuration, payment, and fulfillment branches can apply?
Which transitions are valid, conditional, legacy, or unreachable?
Which source files prove each sequence?

The unit of coverage is a **unique behavior branch**, not every Cartesian permutation. A sequence is unique when it changes an API, validation rule, state transition, money path, issued outcome, or recovery path.
Backend source of truth: [[01 Repositories/Backend - web-app]]
Frontend reachability: [[01 Repositories/Frontend - showpass-frontend]]
Backend purchase overview: `web-app/docs/systems/ticket_basket_purchase_flow.md`
Backend basket state: `web-app/docs/systems/ticket_baskets_checkout_state_and_inventory.md`
Frontend route inventory: `showpass-frontend/packages/next-app/pages/`
Frontend generated platform/item/gateway enums: `showpass-frontend/packages/core/src/shared/constants/enums/index.ts`
Frontend public wizard: `showpass-frontend/packages/core/src/app-contexts/public/features/checkout/`
Build a sequence key from the applicable dimensions:
`ENTRY -> ITEM -> CONFIG -> IDENTITY -> BASKET -> CHECKOUT -> PAYMENT -> FINALIZE -> OUTCOME -> POST`
Example:
`E-WEB-EVENT -> I-TICKET -> C-GA -> A-GUEST -> B-PENDING -> C-QUESTIONS -> P-STRIPE-CARD -> F-WEBHOOK -> O-INVOICE+TICKET -> X-CONFIRMATION`

Use the IDs in the matrices below. Optional stages can be omitted. Interruptions such as login, password access, expiry, provider redirect, or inventory revalidation are inserted where they actually occur.

Source-mapped means the entry, branch rules, purchase/finalization command, outcome, and negative boundary are supported by the referenced matrix/group rows and Evidence Index. It does not claim that a live provider/device/browser run was performed.
Execution pending means source behavior is mapped but an environment-backed E2E, race, worker, rendered-artifact, or provider assertion remains in Open Gaps.

Candidate/unreachable/dormant is reserved for a declared platform or model contract with no current consuming route/service, or for an intentionally impossible combination. It is not counted as a reachable purchase flow.
A sequence row can cite a family rather than repeat every file path; its IDs resolve to the relevant grouped acceptance contract, configuration row, invalid rule, and backend/frontend anchor below.

The diagram deliberately treats identity and provider authorization as conditional interruptions. They are not assumed to occur only once or at a universal fixed step.

Route registrations: `web-app/apps/users/api/routers.py`, `web-app/apps/venues/api/routers.py`, and `web-app/apps/main/api/routers.py`. Actions: `web-app/apps/tickets/api/user_based/viewsets.py` and `web-app/apps/tickets/api/venue_based/viewsets/viewsets.py`.
Backend purchase outcomes and the generated frontend enum establish these basket item families.
This is the current generated frontend contract. It is valuable for finding impossible combinations, but backend validation still decides final validity.
The public checkout wizard registers steps conditionally; this is not a fixed five-step flow.

Widget routes accept a valid `purchase_source_platform` query value and otherwise default to `psp_widget`. This makes embedded platform attribution configurable and requires backend validation; it is not safe to infer platform only from the URL.
Source: `useCheckoutWizard.ts`, `CheckoutContent.web.tsx`, and the files under `components/CheckoutSteps/`.

Account authentication is an interrupt that can be invoked by several surfaces. It must return to the calling state and operate on the same basket/artifact; it is not a fixed node that always occurs before item selection.
Resolve auth without discarding the caller's basket/artifact identifier.
Refetch the authenticated profile rather than trusting only a popup/native success signal.
Update or reload the existing basket customer and auth-dependent pricing, limits, credits, methods, and memberships.
Resume the invoking step: selection gate, login step, transfer accept, account action, code application, or payment return.
Revalidate inventory, hold expiry, access, totals, and provider state because they may have changed during the interrupt.
Do not create a second basket merely because identity changed.

Claim is post-purchase ownership migration, not another payment. A successful claim atomically moves the basket, invoice, ticket items/groups, members and their unowned benefit hold baskets, protection record, voucher credits, and shipping-address creator to the matching account. It also creates virtual-session purchaser participation where required.
The current public checkout selects a business handler first, then a concrete gateway. This prevents invalid combinations even when both values exist independently in generated enums.

recordIds: Provider success followed by Showpass post-payment failure

sections.name: Provider Failure And Replay Acceptance Groups
breadcrumb: Provider Failure And Replay Acceptance Groups

content.text: Checkout delivery is evaluated per item group, not once per basket. Mixed baskets can therefore contain several delivery selections and one shared shipping address requirement.
Delivery method selection is required only for eligible item groups whose allowed methods are unresolved; public `st_print` alone is treated as electronic delivery without a shipping-mode decision.
A delivery-selected item makes shipping address validation required at payment.
Venue allowed-country, blocked-region, and shipping-quantity settings can invalidate an otherwise purchasable basket.
A basket over its shipping quantity limit must have valid fallback delivery modes for affected items; otherwise backend validation rejects it.
Transfer acceptance can independently prompt the recipient for updated shipping information when transferred items require it.
`Allowed` means the source proves the combination can coexist at basket level. It does not imply every platform, discount, gateway, or delivery combination is valid.
Current source uses “upgrade” for a mutable-cart upsell. It is not an action on a paid TicketItem/Member and does not create exchange credit or a source-invoice adjustment.
User credit requires a system gateway; a custom gateway is rejected.
Ordinary user/gift credit requires authenticated ownership, except seller contexts and the explicit box-office physical gift-card code-only path.
Referral credit and exchange credit have distinct identity/context checks and are not interchangeable with universal user credit.
Credit amount must be positive and cannot exceed the validated usable balance/application rules.
A partial credit leaves the basket on a normal payment type; only complete coverage results in backend `USER_CREDIT` or `EXCHANGE_CREDIT` tender.

Price tiers are backend-owned price/fee snapshots for a TicketType, ProductAttribute, or MembershipLevel. They are not public checkout choices and are distinct from the organization's commercial subscription/pricing tier.

Canonical tier order: `venue flag -> sellable save/enablement generation -> published tier matching current item price -> basket price and fee resolution -> basket retains matching tier -> purchase locks tier -> invoice and adjustment rows preserve tier`.

These acceptance groups replace a single "verify subtotal/fees/total" check. Each group owns a distinct calculation or reachability contract and can be traced independently through the sequences and invalid cases below.

`initialize shipping -> resolve base price/subtotal_1 -> allocate discount -> calculate fees/taxes and subtotal_2 (including shipping) -> allocate one credit family -> roll up/round totals -> choose or refresh tender/provider amount -> assert invoice/provider reconciliation`

This order is behavioral. A basket mutation that changes quantity, discount, delivery, credit, payment type, platform, or seller context must repeat the affected downstream stages; clients must not keep an earlier PaymentIntent/session amount.

Tips are not a normal pre-checkout basket choice in the inspected web/mobile code. The proven implementation derives the amount from Square's completed transaction and adds the financial group during purchase reconciliation.
Basket status is authoritative backend state. `abandoned` is not a `TicketBasket.status`; it is a recoverability artifact created after an eligible basket becomes `expired`.
Every persisted add, quantity change, removal, upgrade replacement, seat/location edit, and dependent-package rebuild follows the same acceptance order:

`active mutable basket -> current checkout key + row lock -> normalize requested groups -> rebuild dependencies -> identify deleted/detached rows -> save final group/usages shape -> release removed reservations -> lock and reserve positive deltas -> enforce limits -> recalculate modifiers/totals -> rotate key -> return authoritative basket`
These are canonical representatives. More sequences will be added as configuration rules are verified.

headers: Resulting state or new commerce entry

content.text: Evaluate these groups in order for every post-purchase action. An action is reachable only when every applicable group accepts; later side effects never compensate for a failed earlier gate.
Canonical post-purchase decision order:

`artifact/source links -> actor/venue authority -> locked lifecycle state -> payment/remaining value -> protection -> external integration -> action-specific transition -> dependent side effects -> invoice/item/history reconciliation -> refreshed action eligibility`

Conflict precedence is state-based, not button-based: an accepted claim/transfer/resale/refund/void/dispute transition writes the state that blocks incompatible operations. Read-only view, resend, download, and history can remain available where their artifact/status rules allow.

sections.name: Refund Branches That Must Remain Distinct
breadcrumb: Refund Branches That Must Remain Distinct

content.text: Full, full without charges, full without company charges, company-charges-only, and partial refunds use different formulas.
Partial refund is more restricted and targets one invoice item at a time.
Refund-to-credit creates a ledger outcome rather than reversing the original tender.
Some gateway refunds remain in progress until a provider callback; Square terminal/QPOS has its own rules.
Full refunds are blocked when protection items have claimed coverage.
Credit-paid purchases and code-only gift-card/card splits have special restrictions; the latter is rollout-controlled.
Payout-completed items can be blocked when the venue does not allow refunds after payout.
External supplier and ResDiary orders have booking-specific refund/partial-refund guards.

sections.name: Void Branches That Must Remain Distinct
breadcrumb: Void Branches That Must Remain Distinct

content.text: Full invoice-item void, selected-ticket void, and selected-member void are separate selections.
Void changes sold item state and can create void invoice lines; it is not merely a zero-dollar refund.
Void side effects can include system resale, ticket-credit cancellation, protection cancellation, membership renewal rollback, booking cleanup, and asynchronous inventory refresh.
ResDiary partial void is rejected because only full booking cancellation is supported.

These are branch-shaping configurations found in the backend model. Cosmetic/report-only fields are intentionally excluded unless they change selection, validation, pricing, issuance, fulfillment, or post-purchase behavior.
Every ticket configuration maps into the same ordered journey; a branch may skip a stage, but it cannot bypass a later authoritative guard.

headers: Values / modes that produce unique behavior

content.text: These rows separate primary offers from secondary financial lines. A generated platform declaring an item type proves that the platform contract recognizes the type; it does not prove that the item can initiate a basket by itself.

headers: Values / modes that produce unique behavior

content.text: Each checkbox requires backend validation evidence plus frontend reachability or an explicit API/system-only classification.

General admission, concrete assigned seat, GA location/capacity, manual/best-available/full-location/dynamic-hold seating, attraction event/single-event/direct-ticket/product/membership/redirect and time-slot calendars, recurring child, patron dependency, and package parent/child selection (grouped journey and source reachability mapped; multi-client map/calendar/scanner/fulfillment/finance E2E remains)

Free, fixed price, PWYC base/custom/free/invalid, backend-owned price-tier generation/resolution/snapshot/lock/adjustment lifecycle, discount, password scope/literal/regex/window/session/cap/bypass, voucher-only access, member/benefit access, comp, and payment plan (browser/provider cross-product remains; presale fields classified dormant)

Public/hidden/sellers/scheduled visibility, on-sale/off-sale and seller bypass, sold out, legacy/distributed/event/seat/location inventory, group-sale, public/box-office min/max, per-user account/email, organization/employee seller limits, permission reserve/block/sell/release, and held inventory (contracts and entry reachability mapped; distributed and multi-client race execution remains)

Native/distributed inventory truth, mutable reservations, seller/dynamic/waitlist holds, queue fulfillment, public/waitlist resale matching, cancellation/failure rollback, and released/expired inventory

Basket-level purchaser vs per-attendee information, standard vs enhanced fields, item-scoped custom questions, package inheritance, public/box-office enforcement, and flow-specific guest-data bypasses
Event/membership terms acceptance and payment-venue clickwrap/browserwrap consent snapshots, including mapped email/SMS preferences
Electronic/print, will-call, and delivery catalogs; mixed per-group selection; shipping-address destination rules; and package-aware venue shipping caps
Purchaser full-name/email/phone validation and normalization, manual/same-as-shipping/saved/provider billing sources, blocked-country validation, and ticket-name derivation/edit boundaries
Barcode delivery inheritance, immediate/delayed/customer activation, generated/external/universal credentials, package credential counts, and activation/transfer boundaries
Delivery artifact initialization, item/invoice partial and complete fulfillment roll-up, and seller conversion from physical delivery to electronic

Ticket preset/custom package, exact required choices, reverse/nested children, bundled products, optional sub-options, in-basket upgrade, exchange credit, and transfer acceptance boundaries (contracts mapped; package selector/scan/finance and transfer acceptance E2E remain)

sections.name: Products, Memberships, Gift Cards, Donations, Protection
breadcrumb: Products, Memberships, Gift Cards, Donations, Protection

content.text: Product standalone/widget/site/attraction vs event/membership/seller/hold add-on; allow/include/exclude scope; multi-variant quantity/inventory/limit; tier/fees; delivery/fulfillment; transfer; ResDiary/integration; package child; terms/wallet/comms (grouped contracts and current reachability mapped; absent organizer controls explicitly classified)

Membership new/split/grouped purchase; one-time/lifetime/rolling/hard-window/seasonal expiry; renewal pause; immediate/deferred seasonal renewal; level switch/upgrade; access/discount/issue-ticket benefits; generated hold/ticket; assigned seating; fulfillment; transfer; and package/substitute (grouped contracts and current reachability mapped; partial organizer controls explicitly classified)
Gift-card configured denomination (no current freeform amount), single/multiple recipient, purchaser vs recipient, scheduled/immediate email, account redemption vs box-office code use

Donation event/venue source, suggested preset/custom/zero amount, current standalone unreachability, public/seller/hold add-on, currency/processor/fee behavior, invoice reporting, and non-refundable lifecycle

Protection in-cart vs tokenized post-purchase upsell, feature/gateway/item/time/amount eligibility, opt-in/out/error, quote consistency, idempotent basket create/re-entry, linked invoice/policy, claim/status history, and transfer/refund restrictions
Pending, selection required, purchase in progress, on hold, paid, expired, stuck/reset, provider-pending, expiry/extension, idempotency, and abandoned-recovery states

Single item, mixed item, removed/pruned item, requested-zero removal versus operational-zero lines, quantity-limit boundaries, package/seat dependency cleanup, stale/concurrent update rejection, legacy/distributed reservation races, and atomic rollback (browser/database race E2E remains)

Fees, taxes, all-in/absorbed pricing, regular/tiered/member/auto/referral/voucher discounts, gift-card/venue/universal/exchange credit priority, partial/full credit, provider-derived tip, shipping, legacy/itemized/mixed calculation, and tender recalculation (browser/provider cross-product remains an E2E execution gap, not an unmapped contract)

Tracking/referral/source attribution, abandoned-cart reconstruction, idempotent retry, duplicate submit, payment failure, provider return, and webhook replay across current Stripe, Yuno, Authorize.Net, Square terminal/QPOS, and Shift4 contracts (live provider/device execution and historical clients remain environment/E2E gaps)

Invoice/order, TicketItem, Member/benefits, UserCredit/gift card, waitlist subscriber, resale payout, protection policy, and delivery status (artifact/state groups mapped; rendered and worker E2E remains)

Confirmation page/email/SMS, guest order staging, claim/connect, account order detail, download/wallet, resend, and scan/entry (reachability and state contracts mapped; wallet/scanner/device E2E remains)

Refund, void, exchange, transfer, resale, renewal, dispute/chargeback, settlement/payout, and reporting reconciliation; “upgrade” was reclassified as an in-basket upsell (16 post-purchase groups plus 7 upgrade groups mapped; provider/finance concurrency execution remains)
These are provisional until backend validation is inspected.

recordIds: Tip as a standalone catalog purchase
Saved payment method for anonymous guest
Public login step for group sale
Public custom-questions step for group sale

content.text: Live validation execution across every generated platform/item/payment declaration and feature-flag state remains impractical as a Cartesian suite. The unique validation branches are mapped; use the platform, item, payment, incompatibility, and sequence IDs to generate risk-based combinations.

Some branch-shaping Product and advanced Membership settings are backend/API-configurable but have no current organizer control in the inspected frontend. They are explicitly classified in `PCFG-*`, `MCFG-*`, `PRODUCT-*`, `MEMBER-*`, `INV-414`, and `INV-428`; deployment/admin-created configurations still need browser consumption tests.

Exact external-client/version reachability for old QPOS, Facebook MPP, and Google Reserve. Interac, Afterpay, Beanstream, and Braintree are now classified as dormant/historical for current checkout, but deployment-level route proof remains out of scope for source inspection alone.

Browser/native E2E proof for the mapped authentication interrupt contract: password reset/signup, Google/Facebook popup, SAML full-page mobile return, native cookie/session reload, account return URLs, provider-return auth changes, and expiry.

Seller gift-card + ordinary-item reachability is mapped in `MIX-009`/`T-251`; public-web mixed creation and package/resale/exchange combinations still need execution proof. Gift cards intentionally disable seller hold/group-sale conversion (`INV-431`). Upgrade pricing/mutation is mapped, but assigned-seating membership filtering remains a frontend gap and membership-to-ticket is model-only. Square QPOS is the only current tip creator; terminal/kiosk/Shift4 declarations are classified dormant (`INV-432`).

Live-provider/device proof remains for Authorize.Net transient/stale Accept.js recovery and fraud review, Stripe/Yuno redirect/webhook ordering, Square terminal cancel-versus-complete and missing-payment reconciliation, Shift4 device/API failures, and Square QPOS dead-letter recovery. Source contracts are mapped; dormant Beanstream/Braintree/Interac/Afterpay client versions remain historical deployment gaps.

Post-purchase eligibility is mapped through `POST-*`, `T-193` through `T-197`, `T-199` through `T-210`, and `INV-321` through `INV-350`; `T-198` is the corrected in-basket upgrade flow. Remaining proof is browser/device/provider/worker execution for claim re-entry, package and multiscan admission, transfer return with assigned seats, protection claims, Square async refund reset, payment-plan retries, disputes, fulfillment concurrency, and settlement reconciliation.

Every canonical sequence is source-mapped through composite entry/configuration/group/evidence rows. Full live proof remains for representative chains that combine entry, mutation, provider completion, issuance, and retry/negative behavior in one environment-backed run.

Basket updates without a `checkout_idempotency_key` are currently logged but accepted; determine whether deployed clients still require this compatibility branch before treating missing-key rejection as an intended invariant.

Dynamic custom-question enforcement is narrower than the frontend contract: backend validation runs only for submitted responses on box-office-enforced event groups, and `validate_dynamic_form_guest_info_response()` returns on the first empty optional answer instead of continuing to later required fields. Confirm intended API enforcement and add a regression test before treating required-question completion as universal.

Browser E2E proof remains for per-attendee quantity edits, merged mixed-item question applicability, billing-address-as-shipping fallback, destination aliases/subdivision normalization, and package-aware shipping-cap fallback selection.

Customer-barcode activation has a frontend/backend eligibility mismatch: UI requires future paid customer-activation tickets, while the API validates only owned inactive tickets from one basket and does not compare the complete requested ID set. Confirm intended server contract before automating negative activation cases.
External/universal/package credential rendering and scanning still need E2E proof across confirmation PDF, account order, wallet, transfer/return, and scanner clients.

Account name editing has a frontend/backend eligibility mismatch: frontend blocks inactive, past, virtual, transfer/resale-in-progress, transfer-created, and transfer-disabled tickets; backend permits any owned paid ticket when the venue setting is enabled. Confirm intended server contract.

Billing E2E proof remains across direct card, Affirm, stored card with and without an address, same-as-shipping toggles, Yuno manual-address feature flag, Stripe/Yuno provider-return address extraction, automatic guest checkout, and the blocked-country rollout switch.

Waitlist/resale worker E2E proof remains for FIFO/random scheduling, distributed reservation rollback, Stripe SetupIntent/off-session decline, cutoff-bound holds, resale-to-waitlist automation, assigned-location matching, partial submission completion, provider refund failure, banking payout export/import, and concurrent cancel-versus-match races.

Payable-total browser/provider E2E proof remains for apply-once cascade versus proportional rollout, partial itemized apply-to-each row splitting, absorbed fee/tax display, shipping-tax suppression, public credit-priority switching, gift-card claim then spend, exchange overflow outcomes, seller subtotal-override boundaries, and provider amount refresh after each money mutation. The backend contracts and current frontend reachability are mapped in `MONEY-*`, `TCFG-*`, `T-165` through `T-180`, and `INV-281` through `INV-303`.

Basket mutation contracts are mapped in `BMUT-*`, `T-215` through `T-226`, and `INV-361` through `INV-382`. Browser/database race proof remains for stale-key recovery, last-unit legacy/distributed contention, split-display quantity editing, package/seat partial removal, mutation failure rollback, and inventory/config changes between review and purchase.

Price-tier lifecycle is mapped in `TIER-*`, `T-227` through `T-236`, and `INV-383` through `INV-400`. Remaining execution proof is browser/API comparison across ticket/product/member catalog edits, PWYC, held/renewal/package rows, and financial-report exports; the public buyer has no direct tier selector, and dashboard organization pricing-tier gates are unrelated.

records.title: POST /api/user/tickets/baskets/{id}/purchase/
records.summary: Basket access can be proved by session, authenticated owner, or basket UUID. Login is required when the basket disallows guest checkout, except Widget and legacy Facebook-order exceptions. `no_purchase` performs validation only.

records.title: POST /api/user/tickets/baskets/holds/{tracking_link_slug}/purchase/
records.summary: Rejects expired links with `410` when hold expiry is enabled. Login is required when the held basket disallows guest checkout. `no_purchase` validates only.

records.title: POST /api/venue/tickets/baskets/{id}/purchase/
records.summary: Requires seller basket access/box-office permission. `no_purchase` validates only and is not a terminal completion mechanism.

records.title: POST /api/venue/tickets/baskets/{id}/hold/
records.summary: Creates basic/dynamic seller hold; expiry and async creation are feature-switch dependent.

evidence.label: Converges on
evidence.value: Held `TicketBasket` later consumed by user hold or seller flow

records.title: POST /api/user/tickets/baskets/{id}/stripe/payment-intent/
records.summary: Creates/modifies PaymentIntent after basket validation.

evidence.label: Converges on
evidence.value: Stripe webhook re-enters user purchase service as already paid

records.title: POST /api/user/tickets/baskets/{id}/stripe/setup-intent/
records.summary: Sets up an off-session/saved method lifecycle, including waitlist use cases.

evidence.label: Converges on
evidence.value: Saved payment method state; later basket purchase

records.title: POST/PUT /api/user/tickets/baskets/{id}/yuno/checkout-session/
records.summary: Creates/modifies Yuno checkout session and exposes eligible saved methods.

evidence.label: Converges on
evidence.value: Yuno webhook re-enters user purchase service as already paid

records.title: POST /api/user/tickets/baskets/{id}/interac/initial/
records.summary: Legacy/direct Interac initialization still exists in the viewset.

evidence.label: Converges on
evidence.value: User purchase service with Interac charge/gateway data

records.title: GET /api/venue/tickets/baskets/{id}/terminal-status/
records.summary: Classifies persisted terminal state as pending or final; does not itself finalize payment.

records.title: POST /api/user/tickets/baskets/claim/
records.summary: Authenticated claim of a UUID-addressed basket after guest purchase.

records.title: Purchase locks the basket row and rejects an existing `invoice_id`
records.summary: Duplicate/replay attempts must not create a second invoice

records.title: A waitlist basket exits before invoice/payment recording
records.summary: Joining a waitlist is a purchase-command branch but not an ordinary paid invoice sequence

records.title: Webhook purchase marks provider-confirmed basket state before invoice work and skips a second charge
records.summary: Stripe/Yuno provider success must be traced separately from synchronous purchase submission

records.title: Basket and invoice totals are asserted before gateway/persistence completion
records.summary: Every successful trace needs basket-to-invoice reconciliation

records.title: A required shipping address is inserted and validated before final save
records.summary: Product/delivery configurations can block purchase after earlier checkout steps

records.title: Final save attaches invoice, marks basket paid, copies billing/payment state, and rotates the checkout key
records.summary: A completed sequence has one immutable invoice and a frozen paid basket

records.title: Applied venue/exchange/gift-card credit is validated and consumed during save
records.summary: Partial/full credit paths must validate scope, owner, amount, and remaining external tender

records.title: Gift-card credit generation happens after invoice items save
records.summary: Gift-card success requires both paid invoice proof and stored-value proof

records.title: Purchased ticket types, product attributes, membership levels, gift-card denominations, and payment plans are price-locked
records.summary: First purchase can change later editability; sequence verification must use locked purchase values

records.title: Payment validation failure rotates idempotency outside the failed transaction
records.summary: Retry should reuse the basket with a fresh key and still yield at most one paid result

records.title: First Square/Shift4 terminal call can intentionally return without a saved invoice while provider work is pending
records.summary: Terminal initiation and terminal completion are separate states, not one request

records.title: Provider payment can precede invoice/subscription/protection side effects; failures alert and re-raise
records.summary: “Provider succeeded” is not sufficient proof of completed purchase

records.summary: TicketType, ProductAttribute, or MembershipLevel; no target or multiple targets

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: A tier belongs to exactly one supported sellable; generated tiers copy that sellable's venue, and membership seasonal tiers may snapshot the current RenewalSeason

records.title: TIER-GENERATE
records.summary: New sellable, no active tier, unlocked active tier, locked active tier

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Save creates a tier when none exists; matching unlocked tier is reusable/updateable; a locked active tier is immutable and catalog change produces a new tier

records.title: TIER-RESOLVE
records.summary: Multiple historical tiers, unpublished tiers, same price revisited

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Active resolution chooses the newest published tier for that exact sellable and current price; bulk resolution preserves sellable type/ID boundaries

records.title: TIER-BASKET
records.summary: Add ticket/product/member; basket refreshed after catalog price change; renewal target changes

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Enabled venue resolves active tier into item-group price/tier; a matching retained tier keeps that basket's snapshot, but a tier for a different renewed sellable is rejected as stale

records.title: TIER-OVERRIDE
records.summary: PWYC amount, free PWYC, package child, transfer child, payment-plan copy

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Valid PWYC overrides charged base while retaining the matching tier for reporting; child/transfer groups remain zero-priced with tier context; payment-plan installment price has no tier

records.title: TIER-FEE
records.summary: Tier custom/internal fees and fee-calculation config before/after lock

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Basket fee retrieval prefers its tier; unlocked tier uses current venue fee-calculation config, while purchase lock freezes tier price/fee configuration for historical calculation

records.title: TIER-LOCK
records.summary: Purchase by public, venue, Facebook/MPP, or held-basket path

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Purchase locks each root/non-child, non-auto-generated tier once; ticket/product/member sellables are also locked through their ordinary price-lock path

records.title: TIER-INVOICE
records.summary: Sale invoice item and revenue-realization rows

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Purchased item group and invoice item retain the same tier ID; package revenue realization uses tier context without re-resolving today's catalog price

records.title: TIER-ADJUST
records.summary: Refund, void, exchange, dispute, settlement/reporting

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Adjustment/negation rows propagate the source tier ID so historical price/fee cohorts reconcile without being relabeled as the current tier

evidence.label: Primary evidence
evidence.value: refunders, voiders, invoice exchange, dispute and price-tier tests

records.title: MONEY-BASE
records.summary: Fixed/free/PWYC/tier price, quantity, package parent/child, seller subtotal override

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: Resolve and snapshot the authoritative pre-adjustment price; `subtotal_1` is the rounded sum before discount

records.title: MONEY-DISCOUNT-SELECT
records.summary: Regular, voucher credit, tiered, membership, auto-applied, referral; code, generated, or automatic selection

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: Only active, public, in-window, permitted, under-limit discounts attach to eligible groups

records.title: MONEY-DISCOUNT-CALC
records.summary: Amount and percentage; apply-to-each and apply-once; legacy cascade or proportional allocation; itemized partial split

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: Discount is capped to eligible value and fee-safe line floors; it cannot make a line's payable basis negative

records.title: MONEY-FEE
records.summary: Internal company/processing/discovery/resale fees, organizer/custom fees, commission, payment-plan fee; flat/percent and payment/platform rate card

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: Fees are selected from the resolved item, tender, platform, seller, and package context after discount

records.title: MONEY-TAX
records.summary: Item tax, fee tax, shipping tax, absorbed tax, tax-disabled shipping flag

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: Tax uses the post-discount fee basis and the configured taxable components; tax-on-fee stays separately attributable

records.title: MONEY-ABSORB
records.summary: Matched/customer-paid versus organizer-absorbed fee and tax; all-in presentation

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: Absorption changes accounting/display allocation, not the externally payable total; display fields exclude hidden absorbed amounts

records.title: MONEY-SHIPPING
records.summary: Per-item/per-group/flat override, parent-child de-duplication, selected delivery, quantity-cap suppression, optional shipping tax

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: Charge shipping once for the authoritative delivery artifact and only while delivery remains selected/allowed

evidence.label: Primary evidence
evidence.value: calculator shipping initialization/override; shipping limit service

records.title: MONEY-CREDIT-SELECT
records.summary: Exchange, gift card, venue credit, universal credit; public priority and manual toggle

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: One credit family is selected per basket in current public checkout: exchange > gift card > venue > universal

records.title: MONEY-CREDIT-CALC
records.summary: Available balance, venue/currency/identity/source scope, amount requested, partial/full coverage

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: Validate/cap usable balance, allocate only to purchased groups after fees/taxes, and never reduce amount due below zero

records.title: MONEY-EXCHANGE
records.summary: Source invoice, equal/greater replacement, overflow, itemized per-item allocation, full-credit fee context

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: Preserve source tender/fee context; settle the source credit once and route permitted surplus explicitly

records.title: MONEY-TOTAL
records.summary: Legacy or itemized group roll-up, mixed merge, rounding residual, commission, financing and payment-plan variants

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: `total_price = subtotal_2 + service_fees + taxes - credit_applied + applicable commission`; each Decimal component merges exactly once

records.title: MONEY-DISPLAY
records.summary: Item/subtotal/discount/credit/fee/tax/shipping rows, checkout due, recurring plan total, confirmation/invoice

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: UI renders backend `display_*`/total fields and never recomputes provider amount from labels; invoice assertions reconcile the paid amount

evidence.label: Primary evidence
evidence.value: frontend basket fee helpers/checkout summary; purchase-service total assertions

records.title: MONEY-TENDER
records.summary: Positive, zero, partial-credit, full-credit, comp, seller tender, provider-specific financing total

details.label: Required invariant
details.value: Re-evaluated amount selects free/full-credit/normal/provider tender and updates or clears incompatible provider state

records.title: TIP-001
records.summary: Square QPOS transaction returns one or more tenders with `tip_money`

details.label: Backend transition
details.value: Sum tender tips, convert cents to dollars, call `TicketBasket.add_tip()` before invoice finalization

details.label: Accounting outcome
details.value: Quantity-one tip group with `quantity_included_in_total=0`; no TicketItems

records.title: TIP-002
records.summary: Square QPOS transaction has no positive tender tip

records.title: TIP-003
records.summary: Provider total includes a tip

details.label: Backend transition
details.value: Compare `provider final amount - provider tip` to the pre-tip basket total

details.label: Accounting outcome
details.value: Prevents treating the tip as catalog subtotal or accepting a payment for a different basket

records.title: TIP-004
records.summary: Tip is available during the initial provider lookup

details.label: Backend transition
details.value: Tip group finalizes into the same sale invoice

details.label: Accounting outcome
details.value: `Invoice.tip_money` and a separate tip InvoiceItem reconcile with final amount; organizer revenue remains separate

records.title: TIP-005
records.summary: Initial Square lookup fails and dead-letter recovery is enabled

details.label: Backend transition
details.value: Local purchase may complete without tip; later provider recovery creates a related invoice containing the recovered tip

details.label: Accounting outcome
details.value: Original sale remains unchanged and related tip invoice restores eventual accounting consistency

records.title: TIP-006
records.summary: Internal fees apply to a tip

details.label: Backend transition
details.value: Tip fees are absorbed; customer-added tip total is not increased after device confirmation

details.label: Accounting outcome
details.value: Tip amount is reduced by applicable absorbed fees; tip is excluded from organizer item revenue

records.title: TIP-007
records.summary: Square/Shift4 terminal and kiosk platform declarations include `tip`

details.label: Backend transition
details.value: Platform contract permits the item family

details.label: Accounting outcome
details.value: User/device trigger, provider payload, and recovery behavior still need provider-specific proof beyond Square QPOS

records.title: BSTATE-PENDING
records.summary: `pending` (default mutable basket)

details.label: Allowed next behavior
details.value: Add/remove/update groups, extend expiry, put on hold, join a waitlist, or begin purchase

details.label: Inventory and client consequence
details.value: Reserves quantity/seating while mutable; frontend persists and rehydrates it

records.title: BSTATE-SELECTION
records.summary: selection_required

details.label: Allowed next behavior
details.value: Complete custom-package, seat, location, or product-variant choices; serializer returns to `pending` only after all required selections resolve

details.label: Inventory and client consequence
details.value: Still expirable and inventory-holding; purchase validation rejects unresolved selection

records.title: BSTATE-PIP
records.summary: purchase_in_progress

details.label: Allowed next behavior
details.value: Provider confirmation/webhook -> `payed`; explicit/client failure reset -> `pending` or metadata-backed `on_hold`; stale recovery reconciles, cancels, expires, or finalizes

details.label: Inventory and client consequence
details.value: Basket updates and a second purchase are blocked while provider outcome is uncertain; inventory remains held

records.title: BSTATE-HOLD
records.summary: `on_hold` plus hold metadata/link and optional `hold_expiry_date`

details.label: Allowed next behavior
details.value: Seller/user purchase -> `payed`; release/hold expiry -> `expired`; payment failure can restore `on_hold`

details.label: Inventory and client consequence
details.value: Ordinary expiry worker excludes holds; held inventory, seats, customer/group-sale identity, and provider reset fields have distinct lifecycle

records.title: BSTATE-WAITLIST
records.summary: `waitlisted` after purchase command exits early

details.label: Allowed next behavior
details.value: Queue selection can create a separate held purchase opportunity; cancellation/expiry/fulfillment is subscriber-driven

details.label: Inventory and client consequence
details.value: No invoice is created by the join command; ordinary basket editing and expiry are not the completion model

records.title: BSTATE-PAID
records.summary: `payed` with non-null invoice

details.label: Allowed next behavior
details.value: Read/claim/fulfill or enter an eligible post-purchase action; never return to mutable checkout

details.label: Inventory and client consequence
details.value: Backend requires an invoice and rotates the checkout key; frontend treats the basket as terminal and clears persisted active-cart state

records.title: BSTATE-EXPIRED
records.summary: expired

details.label: Allowed next behavior
details.value: Start a new basket or enter eligible abandoned-cart reconstruction; never revive the expired basket in place

details.label: Inventory and client consequence
details.value: Releases item quantities, seats/locations, discounts, resales, exchange credit, distributed inventory, gift-card recipient reservations, and external placeholder booking

records.title: BSTATE-EXPIRY
records.summary: Ten-minute default or sixty-minute extended `expiry_date`; hold and waitlist exceptions

details.label: Allowed next behavior
details.value: Any ordinary save recalculates expiry; eligible customer can request more time through a no-item basket update; async payment preparation enables extended expiry

details.label: Inventory and client consequence
details.value: Frontend timer clears local state at deadline and rehydrate clears any non-active server status; provider return parameters defer initial rehydrate

records.title: BSTATE-IDEMPOTENCY
records.summary: Row lock plus `checkout_idempotency_key` on basket update; invoice guard and checkout-key rotation on purchase

details.label: Allowed next behavior
details.value: Current key update succeeds and receives a new key; stale key or lock conflict returns a recoverable concurrency error

details.label: Inventory and client consequence
details.value: Prevents two client mutations from silently overwriting inventory/checkout state; a missing update key is currently logged but tolerated

records.title: BSTATE-PROVIDER-PENDING
records.summary: Stripe/Yuno intent or Square/Shift4 terminal state exists without a finalized invoice

details.label: Allowed next behavior
details.value: Poll/webhook/final purchase re-entry, explicit reset on failure, or scheduled PIP recovery

details.label: Inventory and client consequence
details.value: Provider success is not equivalent to local completion; Square terminal pending/completed baskets are protected from ordinary expiry

records.title: BSTATE-ABANDONED
records.summary: Eligible expired basket -> `AbandonedCartEmailRecipient` + tracking link

status.label: Verified backend; frontend tracking entry already mapped

details.label: Allowed next behavior
details.value: Scheduled/manual email or push -> tracking entry -> reconstruct current offer into a new referred basket -> normal purchase

details.label: Inventory and client consequence
details.value: Requires email, venue/user permission, paid item groups, eligible events, and no hold/recovery-loop origin; newer overlapping carts and later purchase invalidate stale recovery

records.title: BMUT-GUARD
records.summary: Pending/selection-required mutable basket, simultaneous saves, stale client state

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: PUT update locks the basket `nowait`; current checkout key succeeds and rotates, stale key/lock collision rejects, PATCH is disabled; missing key remains a logged compatibility bypass

records.title: BMUT-QUANTITY
records.summary: Ticket/product/member/gift-card quantity, per-guest split rows, display quantity

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Requested `quantity` is 1..3000 on persisted rows; operational `quantity_included_in_total` initializes from it, while `display_quantity` can aggregate/suppress split rows and later operational counts can diverge

records.title: BMUT-ZERO
records.summary: Buyer sets editable quantity to zero; waitlist/payment-plan/tip operational lines

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Frontend removes zero/undefined requested groups before persistence; backend does not accept an ordinary persisted `quantity=0` row. Legitimate zero semantics use `quantity_included_in_total=0`, payment-plan copies, or non-admission financial lines without making the row a normal purchasable unit

records.title: BMUT-REMOVE
records.summary: Remove a complete standalone group or the last group

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Omitted saved IDs become deleted rows; deletion releases renewal/external inventory relations, gift-card recipient reservations, resales, discounts, and associated usage. An empty mutable basket may persist/expire, but purchase rejects no items

records.title: BMUT-DEPENDENCY
records.summary: Ticket package parent/children, product package parent/variant children, protected group, split guest rows

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Frontend removes the customer-visible dependency set; backend rebuilds configured children from retained parents and deletes detached descendants. Independently purchased children are retained or filtered according to rollout rules rather than guessed by visual grouping

records.title: BMUT-SELECTION
records.summary: Concrete seats/locations, custom-package choices, product preset/variant, delivery selection

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Removing or changing the selection releases old seat/location usages/permissions, reserves the new set, and leaves the basket `selection_required` until every required target choice resolves

evidence.label: Primary evidence
evidence.value: seating cleanup/releaser; selection state; preset validation

records.title: BMUT-DELTA
records.summary: Add/increase, decrease, removal, same-quantity renewal-level/location change

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Quantity-change map is computed against initial saved values by event, type, membership, product, gift denomination, and location; only positive deltas require new capacity, while decreases release owned reservations

records.title: BMUT-INVENTORY
records.summary: Legacy counters versus distributed `InventoryItem` rows

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Deleted distributed rows release before deletion; increases atomically take ticket/event/location/permission rows, decreases release them, dynamic-hold children move ownership to/from parent, and legacy inventory locks the base inventory before counting all active operational quantities

records.title: BMUT-LIMITS
records.summary: Event/type/product/member/gift denomination, public/seller/per-user/email/password/location limits

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: After the final basket shape is saved, lock only affected/increased resources and validate authoritative `quantity_included_in_total`; decreases do not fail because unrelated inventory is now sold out

records.title: BMUT-ROLLBACK
records.summary: Inventory exhaustion, invalid dependency/selection, stale key, database contention, external booking failure

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: The atomic save rolls back rows, counters, reservations, usages, and totals; counter manager explicitly rolls back, and lock timeout returns a retry/smaller-quantity message rather than a partial basket

records.title: BMUT-RECALCULATE
records.summary: Any accepted composition/quantity/delivery/discount/credit/protection mutation

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Backend returns the new authoritative groups, shipping state, discounts, fees, taxes, credit, provider amount, expiry, and key; frontend replaces local state and resets failed optimistic quantity

records.title: BMUT-PURCHASE
records.summary: Mutation followed by purchase, or inventory/config changes between review and submit

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Purchase rejects an empty basket and reruns item terms, guest info, preset choice, totals, payment, and issuance guards; held reservations are verified/consumed rather than trusting the last rendered cart

records.title: POST-ARTIFACT
records.summary: Identify the authoritative object before evaluating a button or route

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Basket/order, sale/refund/void/dispute/settlement invoice, invoice item, TicketItem/product, Member/benefit, UserCredit/gift card, transfer, resale submission, protection item, subscription/installment, or fulfillment row

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: Action is scoped to concrete IDs and their source links; display grouping is never treated as ownership

records.title: POST-ACCESS
records.summary: Resolve actor, venue, and ownership authority

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Guest staged token; matching-email claim; account owner; transfer sender/recipient; venue permission; superuser/system/provider callback

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: Read access, customer mutation, seller mutation, and webhook/system execution remain distinct capabilities

records.title: POST-STATE
records.summary: Resolve current lifecycle state under lock where money, ownership, inventory, or usage can change

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Paid/active/inactive; transfer/resale/waitlist/refund in progress; refunded/voided/resold/expired; fulfillment state; subscription/claim/provider state

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: Only transitions allowed from the current state execute; stale UI and duplicate delivery become rejection or idempotent no-op

records.title: POST-PAYMENT
records.summary: Resolve the original and remaining financial context

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Cash/card/free/comp/other/credit/exchange/payment plan; gateway present/refundable; prior refund/void/dispute; remaining `*_stat`; payout generated/not generated

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: Financial action uses the correct refunder/voider/provider/ledger path and never exceeds unsettled or unadjusted value

records.title: POST-PROTECTION
records.summary: Resolve policy and claim state before entitlement/value recovery

details.label: Required branches
details.value: No policy; created/not issued; issued no claim; submitted; approved; denied/cancelled; provider void/cancel

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: Transfer, resale, full refund, and void honor claim conflicts; protection side effects remain linked to covered items

records.title: POST-INTEGRATION
records.summary: Resolve external owner of booking, payment, credential, or fulfillment state

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Native Showpass; ResDiary; external supplier; ConnectNGo; Stripe/Yuno/Square/Authorize/Shift4; external barcode; wallet

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: External capability and all-or-nothing rules run before local terminal state; failure is visible for reconciliation

records.title: POST-DOCUMENT
records.summary: Decide whether the operation is observational or mutating

details.label: Required branches
details.value: View/stage, resend, PDF/download, Apple/Google wallet, receipt/confirmation language

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: Re-render/resend does not create a sale, change ownership, reset usage, or duplicate a receipt unless the explicit action requires it

records.title: POST-ADMISSION
records.summary: Resolve credential activation and usage scope

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Immediate/delayed/customer activation; generated/external/universal; parent vs child package; single/multiscan; membership/daily benefit; event/time/seat/location

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: One accepted scan consumes only its configured usage; activation and fulfillment do not mint a second credential

records.title: POST-OWNERSHIP
records.summary: Resolve whether ownership may move

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Claim unowned order; ticket/product/member transfer; transfer cancel/return; assigned seat/shipping reconciliation; name edit is not ownership

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: Exactly one current owner receives the usable entitlement while purchaser and financial audit identity remain stable

records.title: POST-RECOMMERCE
records.summary: Resolve source-to-new-purchase actions

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Exchange, renewal, resale buyer match, waitlist fulfillment; current “upgrade” is excluded because it replaces an unpaid basket group

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: New basket/invoice retains explicit source linkage; source value/inventory/entitlement is consumed or restored exactly once

records.title: POST-REFUND
records.summary: Resolve refund selection and formula

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Full; full without charges; full without company charges; company charges only; partial one-item; refund-to-credit; synchronous/async; record-only

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: Remaining customer, organizer, company, tax, fee, shipping, credit, and provider values reconcile to refund invoices and item states

records.title: POST-VOID
records.summary: Resolve non-refund cancellation scope

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Full invoice-item, selected tickets, selected members; renewal member; credits; system resale; protection; booking; async inventory update

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: Sold items become voided/resold with void-side accounting and dependent cleanup, without implying tender reversal

records.title: POST-RECURRING
records.summary: Resolve ongoing membership/payment-plan eligibility

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Renewable vs lifetime/one-time; immediate vs seasonal rollover; installment due/retry; subscription cancel/refund; plan refresh

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: Each renewal/installment is a source-linked commerce event and benefits/schedules advance once

records.title: POST-FULFILLMENT
records.summary: Resolve physical work independently from payment/ownership

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Not applicable, not fulfilled, partially fulfilled, fulfilled; delivery/pickup; conversion to electronic

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: Item changes roll up to invoice status; repeated or terminal-item fulfillment cannot reopen completed work

records.title: POST-DISPUTE
records.summary: Resolve provider-driven involuntary adjustment

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Charge mapping, status/reason/deadline/amount, prior refunds/exchanges, dispute fee, system resale/item action

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: One dispute accounting chain and fee set is linked to one sale; missing/ambiguous mapping alerts

records.title: POST-SETTLEMENT
records.summary: Resolve payout eligibility only after prior adjustments

details.label: Required branches
details.value: Payout date, unsettled source row, `*_stat` values, item category, gateway, refund/void/dispute/exchange/resale/plan effects

details.label: Stable outcome
details.value: Settlement invoices aggregate each eligible source row once and link it back for later refund blocking/reconciliation

records.title: TICKET-DISCOVER
records.summary: Public detail/discovery/org/site/widget/calendar; seller web/desktop/mobile/POS/kiosk; account renewal/exchange; waitlist/resale/hold/system feeder

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: The source resolves an event/occurrence and visible seller-appropriate TicketType, or explicitly classifies the offer unreachable; source/platform attribution survives into the basket

evidence.label: Primary evidence
evidence.value: entry inventory; public/seller querysets and routes

records.title: TICKET-AUTHORIZE
records.summary: Guest/account/seller/system identity; event/type password; member/voucher/discount/hold/resale permission

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Authorization reveals or reserves only its scoped ticket inventory and is rechecked on basket update/purchase; an auth interruption can resume the same basket

records.title: TICKET-SELECT
records.summary: GA quantity; concrete seat; GA location; best available; attraction/recurring occurrence; patron option; package/custom/product child

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Selection resolves one concrete sellable shape and every required seat/location/choice before purchase; unresolved work keeps `selection_required`

records.title: TICKET-COMPOSE
records.summary: Standalone, package parent/child, optional sub-option, membership substitute, add-ons, upgrade replacement, mixed basket

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Dependency expansion creates only configured child/artifact rows at the correct ratios; basket mutation rebuilds or removes the whole owned dependency set

records.title: TICKET-VALIDATE
records.summary: Visibility/window; inventory; public/seller/customer/password limits; terms/info/consent; delivery; integration

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Final basket shape is validated against current source truth under required locks; stale discovery or UI eligibility never authorizes purchase

evidence.label: Primary evidence
evidence.value: basket serializers, limit/reservation services, purchase serializer

records.title: TICKET-PRICE
records.summary: Fixed/free/PWYC/tier; discounts/fees/taxes/shipping/credit; comp/payment plan/provider amount

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Backend derives the authoritative payable total after every composition/context mutation and snapshots the price/tier/fee lineage used for the invoice

records.title: TICKET-FINALIZE
records.summary: Free/credit/card/seller/provider/deferred path; sync/async/replay

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Exactly one successful command converts the locked basket to an invoice or the explicit waitlist-subscriber outcome; provider success without local finalization enters reconciliation

records.title: TICKET-ISSUE
records.summary: TicketItem/barcode, package credential, external/universal code, membership substitute, integration/fulfillment

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Issued artifacts match operational quantity, selected occurrence/seat/location, delivery/activation and owner; no hidden child or substitute issues an unintended ordinary ticket

records.title: TICKET-LIFECYCLE
records.summary: Scan/activation, transfer, resale, exchange, refund, void, fulfillment, dispute, settlement

details.label: Acceptance contract
details.value: Every later action starts from the issued invoice/item and preserves source, tier, ownership, inventory, credential and accounting linkage exactly once
